test.name: Write a proved-tier proof for the completed father-identification research
test.description: The flynn-research-complete-no-proof scenario has all planned searches completed including a negative probate result. The skill should produce a `proved` tier proof — research is reasonably exhaustive (per the populated stop_criteria in q_001) and five independent original sources converge, with TWO of them providing primary information on the father-child relationship: the 1845 Irish Catholic baptism and the contemporaneous family Bible birth entry. The 1850/1860 censuses provide corroborating indirect evidence (household position, no explicit relationship column until 1880) and the 1908 death certificate is direct from a secondary informant. This two-primary-source basis is what clears the skill's own 'proved = 2+ independent original sources with primary information' bar; tests the high-confidence tier path, distinct from the `probable` tier the seed test covers.

input.user_message: Write the final proof conclusion for Patrick Flynn's parentage.

judge_context: Should select the `proved` tier and justify it: the search is reasonably exhaustive (q_001's `exhaustive_declaration` is populated with stop_criteria, all planned searches including the negative probate complete) and TWO independent original sources provide primary information on the parent-child relationship — the 1845 Irish baptism (a_014) and the contemporaneous family Bible birth entry (a_015) — meeting the 'proved = 2+ independent original sources with primary information' bar, corroborated by the 1850/1860 census co-residence and the 1908 death certificate
Should incorporate the negative probate result (log_006) into the narrative — exhaustive search includes documenting what was searched and didn't turn up, not just what did
Should cite the resolved birthplace conflict c_001 within the narrative — proof conclusions don't omit resolved conflicts; they're part of the GPS audit trail showing the analytical work

The proof presented in the reply should cite the supporting sources with their classifications — the 1850 census, 1860 census, 1908 death certificate, 1845 baptism, and the family Bible — such that a reader without research.json can follow it
